title: TrustLens finding
description: One check result about one capability. A finding is always a statement about a recorded scope under a recorded rule; it is never a statement about the artifact as a whole.

finding_id.description: `<component>:<capability>:<16 hex>` where the hex is the deterministic digest defined in SCHEMA.md. Same inputs must yield the same id across runs, so that two runs can be diffed.

rule_id.description: Identifier of the rule, analysis, or external check that produced this finding. Every finding has one, including declared-surface extractions.

rule_version.description: Version of that rule. Changing a rule's behavior requires changing this, otherwise two incomparable results can share an identifier.

evidence_unavailable_reason.description: Only for a FOUND result where no location can be produced (e.g. a behavior observed by a monitor with no file coordinate). Null otherwise. A FOUND finding with neither evidence nor this reason is invalid.

confidence_basis.description: Why this finding is believed to the degree claimed, in the analyst-readable sense: what the rule matched and what that does and does not imply. Not a numeric score.

limitations.description: What this specific finding does not establish. Required and non-empty: every check has a blind spot, and a finding claiming none is a finding whose blind spot has not been examined.

independently_reproduced.description: Whether a second, independent method reached the same result. Absence of reproduction is normal and is recorded as such rather than left blank.

by.description: Independent methods or tools that reproduced it. Empty when reproduced is false.

records.description: Record ids of the corroborating runs.

derived_from.description: finding_ids this finding was composed from. Required non-empty for graph_derivation. A derived finding is never stronger than its weakest input.

environment_description_ref.description: Required (non-null) for config_derivation and policy_evaluation findings, so the capture timestamp travels with the finding rather than living only in the run header.

allOf.$comment: LOAD-BEARING. A clean-scope result may not coexist with an incomplete scope. Any failed item forces PARTIAL, so a parse failure can never be reported as a completed clean scan.
LOAD-BEARING. PARTIAL must name what failed and why, otherwise it degrades into an unfalsifiable hedge.
A FOUND result needs a location, or an explicit statement that no location is obtainable.
Method and strength must agree. This prevents a configuration-derived path being recorded with the strength of an observation.
Reproduction claims must name the reproducing method.
